refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall collect and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) to enable p95 KPI calculation.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall not compromise safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop, load drop prevention) in pursuit of cycle time optimization.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The teleoperation subsystem (if active) shall not introduce latency that degrades cycle time below CT_target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/powertrain efficiency and control responsiveness; implement real-time cycle time monitoring and telemetry streaming; establish baseline CT_target via duty cycle simulation.

DP-B: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, dump phases; allocate time budgets to each phase; design vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics, bucket) to meet phase budgets; implement phase-gated telemetry.

DP-C: Implement predictive cycle time modeling (digital twin); use model to identify bottlenecks; optimize vehicle parameters (gear ratios, pump displacement, valve response); validate against field telemetry.
DP-D: Establish cycle time SLA with performance monitoring dashboard; implement automated alerts when p95 drifts above CT_target; enable operator feedback loop for continuous improvement.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, dump phases; allocate time budgets to each phase; design vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics, bucket) to meet phase budgets; implement phase-gated telemetry.

potential_sources: Hydraulic system pressure/flow coupling between load and travel phases (mitigated by phase-gated control logic).
Engine power allocation between propulsion and auxiliary loads (mitigated by load-sensing hydraulics and priority valve design).
Teleoperation latency impact on phase transitions (addressed in FR-NGHE-002.4 sub-requirement).
coupling_check.notes: DP-B is weakly coupled: phase decomposition allows independent optimization of load, travel, and dump subsystems. Coupling is managed via control sequencing and telemetry gating. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (power, hydraulic capacity, structural limits). Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, DP-B will fail at validation. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study early.
Assumption: Phase-gated telemetry can be implemented with <50 ms latency overhead. Risk: Telemetry processing delays could mask true cycle time. Mitigation: Validate telemetry latency in prototype.

Assumption: Operator behavior (load placement, travel path, dump technique) is consistent across duty cycle. Risk: High variance in operator technique could exceed p95 target. Mitigation: Operator training and standardized procedures.

Risk: Safety-critical interlocks (load drop prevention, emergency stop) may add latency that conflicts with cycle time optimization. Mitigation: Design safety logic to operate in parallel with cycle optimization; validate no interference.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly include: (1) phase time budget allocation methodology (e.g., load ≤ T_load, travel ≤ T_travel, dump ≤ T_dump, with T_load + T_travel + T_dump ≤ CT_target); (2) telemetry sampling rate and phase detection algorithm; (3) safety-critical path analysis to confirm no latency injection from safety interlocks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module owns the physical realization of load, travel, and dump cycle execution. This includes the powertrain (engine, transmission), hydraulic system (pump, valves, actuators), bucket/load handling mechanism, and embedded control electronics that orchestrate phase transitions and report phase timestamps via telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle emits cycle phase timestamps (load_start, load_end, travel_start, travel_end, dump_start, dump_end) and performance metrics (actual cycle time, phase durations, engine load, hydraulic pressure) to enable p95 KPI calculation and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives operational commands (load, travel, dump) from operator or teleoperation subsystem; tasking includes phase sequencing and any performance mode hints (e.g., 'optimize for speed' vs. 'optimize for fuel').

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives safety-critical alerts (emergency stop, load drop prevention trigger, stability warning) from Safety Services; these may interrupt or override cycle phase execution.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container but does not directly own cycle time execution logic. Cycle time is primarily a Vehicle responsibility. Assigning to NGHE Solution would diffuse ownership and obscure accountability. Not recommended as primary IM.

Vehicle is the natural owner of load, travel, dump cycle execution. All physical subsystems (powertrain, hydraulics, bucket) are contained within Vehicle. Telemetry emission and Tasking reception are Vehicle responsibilities. Containment is high; diffusion is minimal. Recommended as primary IM.

Teleoperation is responsible for remote operator interface and command transmission, not cycle execution. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would diffuse responsibility and create false coupling. Teleoperation may influence cycle time via latency (FR-NGHE-002.4) but does not own the execution. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety-critical monitoring and alerts, not cycle time optimization. Assigning cycle time to Safety Services would create inappropriate coupling and diffuse safety focus. Safety Services may constrain cycle time (via SafetyAlert interface) but does not own execution. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module contains all physical and control logic necessary to execute the load→travel→dump cycle within the time budget. Cycle time performance is directly measurable at the Vehicle boundary via Telemetry interface (phase timestamps). Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces provide external constraints and commands that Vehicle must respect while optimizing cycle time.
containment_risks: Risk: Teleoperation latency (if teleoperation is active) may degrade cycle time below CT_target. Mitigation: FR-NGHE-002.4 explicitly addresses this; Teleoperation module must ensure command latency is within acceptable bounds. Vehicle module assumes Tasking commands arrive with <acceptable_latency>.

Risk: Safety Services may impose additional interlocks or delays that increase cycle time. Mitigation: Safety-critical path analysis (FR-NGHE-002.3) must confirm no unacceptable latency injection. Vehicle module must design safety logic to operate in parallel with cycle optimization.

Risk: Operator behavior variance may cause p95 cycle time to exceed CT_target even if Vehicle is optimized. Mitigation: This is a duty cycle and training issue, not a Vehicle design issue. Acceptance criteria should account for operator variance or include operator training as a prerequisite.

Risk: Duty cycle assumptions (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions) may not match field reality. Mitigation: Acceptance criteria must specify duty cycle conditions; field validation must confirm CT_target is achievable under actual site conditions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the boundary between Vehicle cycle time execution and Teleoperation/Safety Services constraints. Explicitly define: (1) acceptable Tasking command latency; (2) acceptable SafetyAlert response time; (3) duty cycle assumptions (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions); (4) operator training/standardization requirements. These should be documented as assumptions in the Vehicle design specification.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (phase decomposition with time budgets and phase-gated telemetry) directly addresses FR-NGHE-002 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The DP is specific, measurable, and testable. Phase decomposition enables independent optimization of load, travel, and dump subsystems while maintaining overall cycle time control. Telemetry gating provides measurement capability for p95 KPI calculation. No functional gaps detected.

diffusion_risks: Teleoperation latency impact: DP-B assumes Tasking commands arrive with acceptable latency. If Teleoperation introduces >acceptable_latency, cycle time will degrade. Mitigation: Teleoperation module must be designed to meet latency SLA; this is captured in FR-NGHE-002.4.

Safety Services latency impact: DP-B assumes SafetyAlert processing does not introduce unacceptable delays. If Safety Services adds >acceptable_latency, cycle time will degrade. Mitigation: Safety-critical path analysis must confirm parallel execution; this is captured in FR-NGHE-002.3.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is the clear owner of cycle time execution. Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces are well-defined and map to known system interfaces. No diffusion of cycle time responsibility across multiple modules. Containment is high.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is directly measurable via Vehicle telemetry (phase timestamps). p95 calculation is straightforward statistical analysis of field data. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle) is clear and testable. Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Vehicle module is clearly allocated the responsibility for cycle time execution. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through FR-NGHE-002.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services as appropriate. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to include explicit phase time budget allocation methodology and telemetry sampling rate specification.
Clarify duty cycle assumptions (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions) and operator training/standardization requirements in acceptance criteria.
Conduct feasibility study to confirm CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints.
Define acceptable Tasking command latency and SafetyAlert response time; ensure Teleoperation and Safety Services modules meet these SLAs.
Perform safety-critical path analysis to confirm no unacceptable latency injection from safety interlocks.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI requirement that is not atomic. Refinement has identified four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through FR-NGHE-002.4) that address distinct aspects: vehicle cycle execution, telemetry monitoring, safety constraint, and teleoperation latency. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to the next level of decomposition is necessary to fully specify the design and allocate responsibilities across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall collect and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) to enable p95 KPI calculation.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall not compromise safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop, load drop prevention) in pursuit of cycle time optimization.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The teleoperation subsystem (if active) shall not introduce latency that degrades cycle time below CT_target.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (power, hydraulic capacity, structural limits).
Phase-gated telemetry can be implemented with <50 ms latency overhead.
Operator behavior (load placement, travel path, dump technique) is consistent across duty cycle or standardized via training.
Tasking commands from operator/teleoperation arrive with acceptable latency (<acceptable_latency_threshold>).
SafetyAlert processing does not introduce unacceptable delays; safety logic operates in parallel with cycle optimization.
Duty cycle assumptions (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions) match field reality.
p95 KPI is calculated from field telemetry data collected under agreed duty cycle and site conditions.
open_risks: CT_target feasibility: If CT_target is unrealistic, DP-B will fail at validation. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study early.
Teleoperation latency: If Teleoperation introduces >acceptable_latency, cycle time will degrade. Mitigation: Teleoperation module must meet latency SLA (FR-NGHE-002.4).
Safety Services latency: If Safety Services adds >acceptable_latency, cycle time will degrade. Mitigation: Safety-critical path analysis must confirm parallel execution (FR-NGHE-002.3).
Operator behavior variance: High variance in operator technique could exceed p95 target. Mitigation: Operator training and standardized procedures.
Duty cycle mismatch: Field conditions may not match duty cycle assumptions. Mitigation: Acceptance criteria must specify duty cycle conditions; field validation must confirm CT_target is achievable.
evidence_needed: Feasibility study confirming CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints.
Telemetry latency analysis confirming <50 ms overhead.
Teleoperation latency specification and validation confirming <acceptable_latency_threshold>.
Safety-critical path analysis confirming no unacceptable latency injection from safety interlocks.
Duty cycle specification (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions) and operator training/standardization procedures.
Instrumented field trial data (cycle time telemetry, p95 KPI calculation, safety event correlation) confirming acceptance criteria are met.
